era: 1987-1994. Grunge. E=9 -- S36 FIRST MAXIMUM. Aberdeen, Washington to Capitol Hill, Seattle. Sub Pop Records (1988-1990) to DGC Records/Geffen (1991-1994).

location.origin: Aberdeen, Washington / Olympia, Washington / Capitol Hill, Seattle, Washington

members: Kurt Donald Cobain (guitar/vocals, born February 20 1967 Aberdeen WA, died April 5 1994 Seattle WA, age 27)
Krist Novoselic (bass, born May 16 1965 Compton CA, raised Aberdeen WA)
Dave Grohl (drums, born January 14 1969 Warren OH, raised Springfield VA, joined September 1990)
key_albums: Bleach (Sub Pop SP34, June 1989) -- debut LP, Jack Endino, $606.17, Melvins-influenced + About a Girl
Nevermind (DGC, September 24 1991) -- THE record, Butch Vig, Smells Like Teen Spirit, 30M worldwide
In Utero (DGC, September 13 1993) -- Steve Albini, deliberate de-polishing, Heart-Shaped Box, All Apologies
MTV Unplugged in New York (DGC, November 1994, posthumous) -- November 18 1993 recording, most intimate document

theory_nodes.concept: E=9: S36 FIRST MAXIMUM. First time the S36 track reaches E=9 in the entire engine. Calibration: the most important rock band of the 1990s by simultaneous measurement of commercial impact, cultural impact, and artistic quality. Nevermind displaced Michael Jackson from #1; sold 30M worldwide; ended hair metal's commercial dominance; created the 1990s rock market.
theory_nodes.description: Nirvana as S36 first E=9 and the calibration justification.

theory_nodes.concept: THE MELVINS CONNECTION (degree 308): Buzz Osborne introduced Cobain to Pixies/Black Flag/Butthole Surfers/Meat Puppets. First show Cobain ever saw: the Melvins. Cobain tried to join Melvins; rejected; formed Nirvana instead. Dale Crover played the Dale Demo (1988). Grohl cites Crover as primary influence. The Melvins made Nirvana possible.
theory_nodes.description: Complete Melvins-Nirvana relationship chain: education, first show, rejection, Dale Demo, Crover-Grohl.

theory_nodes.concept: Kurt Cobain: born February 20 1967 Aberdeen WA. Died April 5 1994 Seattle WA. Age 27. Chronic stomach pain (never diagnosed). Heroin addiction. Depression. Creative gifts: pop melodies in heavy rock context, guitar sound heavy + melodically legible, lyrics oblique but emotionally direct. The artistry is the complete and lasting record.
theory_nodes.description: Cobain's life, gifts, and death documented with factual respect.

theory_nodes.concept: NEVERMIND (1991): Butch Vig production. Sound City Studios Van Nuys. Smells Like Teen Spirit single September 10 1991. Displaced Michael Jackson Dangerous from #1. January 1992: #1 US. 30 million worldwide. Ended hair metal commercial era. Created the market for 1990s alternative rock. The most commercially impactful rock album since Led Zeppelin.
theory_nodes.description: Nevermind's commercial and cultural impact chain.

theory_nodes.concept: IN UTERO (1993): Steve Albini production. Deliberate correction to Nevermind's polish. DGC tension; Scott Litt re-mixed lead singles. Songs: Frances Farmer, Pennyroyal Tea, Rape Me. Most politically explicit Nirvana record. Cobain's discomfort with commercial success made audible.
theory_nodes.description: In Utero as artistic correction and its production history.

theory_nodes.concept: MTV UNPLUGGED (November 18 1993, released posthumously November 1994): candles and lilies staging. About a Girl. The Man Who Sold the World (Bowie). Where Did You Sleep Last Night (Leadbelly). Meat Puppets covers (Plateau, Oh Me, Lake of Fire) with Curt and Cris Kirkwood. Most emotionally resonant Cobain document. Final image: sustained note, staring at audience. Five months later he was gone.
theory_nodes.description: MTV Unplugged as the definitive Cobain document and its posthumous weight.

theory_nodes.concept: GEOGRAPHIC ARC: Aberdeen (origin, Melvins context, Novoselic and Cobain both Aberdeen) -> Olympia (1988-89, K Records, riot grrrl, Tobi Vail, political formation) -> Seattle Capitol Hill (1989-1994, Sub Pop to DGC, primary scene association). The most geographically complex origin story in the grunge cluster.
theory_nodes.description: Nirvana's geographic arc from Aberdeen periphery to Seattle center.

cross_references.relationship: Necessary precedent. Osborne education. Dale Demo. First show Cobain attended. Rejection forced Nirvana formation. Crover-Grohl influence chain. The Melvins made Nirvana possible.
1989 European co-headline tour. Pre-Nevermind Sub Pop peers. Last moment of equal standing before Nevermind divergence.
Nirvana covered Touch Me I'm Sick on 1990 split single. Cobain-Arm peer relationship. Both Sub Pop. Nirvana left for DGC; Mudhoney eventually stayed.
Both at E=9 in the Summit. Cornell and Cobain were Seattle peers. Both lost to deaths connected to personal pain. Both handled with respect in this engine.
Dave Grohl after Nirvana. The continuation. Grohl is one of the few people to be primary member of two historically significant rock bands.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=9 + E=9 = E=18. THE SUMMIT MAXIMUM. Both tracks at E=9 simultaneously for the first time. Nirvana's multi-voice convergence (Cobain guitar/vocals + Novoselic bass + Grohl drums as unified acoustic event) paired with the Orca pod's full multi-voice reunion greeting ceremony. Both are rare, explosive, full-convergence acoustic events.
